title: What they DON’T teach you in medical school - MUST WATCH

chunks.title: Introduction to Nutrition Education
chunks.summary: The speaker discusses the lack of nutrition education in medical schools, highlighting a personal experience at the University of Arizona. He expresses disappointment in the medical students' understanding of nutrition and believes this gap contributes to broader health issues.
chunks.transcript: doctors don't know nutrition I wasn't taught nutrition in medical school or residency your doctor probably wasn't taught nutrition in medical school or residency and I think this is a huge part of the problem so in this video I went to the University of Arizona where I went to medical school and I talked to medical students we eventually got kicked out because we weren't supposed to do this but I actually was able to have a conversation with some medical students about what they were being taught in nutrition what they thought about red meat what they thought about poly unrated fat seed oils saturated fat and the results were enlightening I tried to mess with them a little bit I tried to teach them a little bit but I think when you see what these medical students say about nutrition and what they're being taught and their understanding you'll understand why your doctor doesn't get it probably so I hope this video will be enlightening to you it was fun to talk to these medical students who agreed to be filmed and share these ideas with you guys but I was a little disappointed in what they knew and what they didn't know and like I said I I think this is a real indication of major problems with the way that we're teaching our medical students in the medical system today and I think this has to change if people are

chunks.title: Understanding Hypertension
chunks.summary: A discussion ensues among medical students about the causes of hypertension, including environmental factors and dietary choices. The role of stress and diet, particularly sodium intake and fatty foods, is highlighted as significant contributors to high blood pressure.

chunks.title: Red Meat and Health Perceptions
chunks.summary: The panel debates the health implications of red meat consumption. Medical students share their beliefs, influenced by educational teachings that frame red meat as harmful, while exploring its relationship with hypertension.

chunks.title: Butter and Fats in Diet
chunks.summary: The conversation shifts to the consumption of butter and other fats, with students acknowledging the potential dangers of excessive intake. They explore how daily dietary choices, such as high-fat meat and butter, might lead to health issues like inflammation and hypertension.

chunks.title: Epidemiology vs. Interventional Studies
chunks.summary: A deeper dive into the types of studies that relate diet to health outcomes forms the crux of a discussion on the validity of observational versus interventional studies. Students are encouraged to understand biases within research and how data contributes to medical understanding.

chunks.title: Personal Dietary Choices and Reflections
chunks.summary: Medical students share personal reflections on their dietary habits, discussing transitions toward healthier eating patterns. This reflects their consideration of cultural influences on diet, as well as an awareness of moderation in meat consumption.

chunks.title: Conclusion on Nutrition Education's Impact
chunks.summary: The speaker concludes by urging a reevaluation of how nutrition is taught in medical schools. Emphasizing the gap in knowledge, he calls for better education in nutrition to improve future healthcare outcomes.
